channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Need a bodyguard? Now there’s an app for that
item.description: A new app makes hiring security as simple as ordering an Uber. A viral TikTok with 11.2 million views reads, “POV we just ordered bodyguards to pick us up from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: White House says Elon Musk is not part of DOGE
item.description: The White House said Monday that Elon Musk is technically not part of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), even though the tech billionaire is said to be leading its sweeping cost-cutting efforts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: DOGE says it has saved government $55B so far
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in a post on its website says it has found $55 billion in savings through a combination of efforts, including a reduction in the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Electric SUV is the ultimate lifeline for search and rescue missions
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about the Genesis GV60 MIV, an electric rescue EV with snow tracks for extreme missions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The iPhone SE 4: A New Era in Mobile Technology?
item.description: Apple's latest iPhone, the SE 4, is rumored to feature a new Apple-designed 5G modem, but will it be up to par with Qualcomm's Snapdragon X75?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Pro-Russian hackers hit Italian bank, airport websites
item.description: The Noname057 hacker group has been linked to recent cyberattacks against Italian banking and airport websites.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3 has finally raced its way onto Paramount Plus, and I'm gutted that I won't be able to stream it
item.description: The third Sonic the Hedgehog movie has made its official streaming debut, but only in certain countries.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Musk’s xAI launches Grok 3, expanding AI capabilities with deep search and reasoning
item.description: Elon Musk’s AI startup xAI has introduced Grok 3, the latest version of its chatbot model, which Musk describes as the most advanced AI system yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Stephen Miller: DOGE will restore 'faith and confidence' in IRS
item.description: White House deputy chief of staff Stephen Miller said Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) will restore “faith and confidence” in the Internal Revenue Service (IRS).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Gen Z isn’t worried about China having access to their data. They should be
item.description: With TikTok and DeepSeek, young people are forking over sensitive personal data to the Chinese government. We should be worried. Among Gen Z, there’s a certain nihilism about China’s access to American data. Some argue that they have nothing to hide.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Links to Signal messaging platform not working on X
item.description: Users on Elon Musk's social media platform X were not able to share or post links to the encrypted messaging app Signal on Monday and were greeted with various failure notifications when they tried to do so.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How to use AI to find new movies, music, and books
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Imagine turning your reading history into a treasure map.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: How DoD modernization can advance mission readiness for the warfighter
item.description: Getting the right technology and data is imperative for driving DoD transformation initiatives, says Maximus’ Bryan Martz. The post How DoD modernization can advance mission readiness for the warfighter first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: WordPress Foundation bid for greater trademark control halted, adding to more legal setbacks for CEO Matt Mullenweg
item.description: WordPress foundation requested to disclaim rights to Hosted WordPress and Managed WordPress, but it's not a denial for the trademarks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appear to gain momentum with a visit by a top Chinese official to the border zone ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: She helped create the U.S. Digital Service. Now it’s become the U.S. DOGE Service
item.description: As former U.S. Deputy Chief Technology Officer, Jen Pahlka helped to create the U.S. Digital Service (USDS). Today, the USDS houses Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), hell-bent on quick, disruptive change.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Juniper CEO: ‘I am disappointed and somewhat puzzled’ by DOJ merger rejection
item.description: Few would have predicted the U.S. Department of Justice would move to block HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. When it happened late last month, the two vendors and many industry watchers were caught off guard.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple’s emotional lamp and the future of robots
item.description: Pixar Animation Studios has an unusual logo. The basic logo is the word “Pixar.” But sometimes, an animated lamp named Luxo Jr. hops into the frame and jumps on the letter “i.” The lap exudes personality and represents Pixar’s ability to turn any object into a compelling character.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI-powered fitness revolutionizing workouts with adaptive equipment
item.description: “I’m a tech nerd really at heart, and it’s cool to be on that cutting edge,” said Trevor Gere, co-owner of The Smart Fit Method in Northwest Suburban Kildeer.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, NewsNation

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dynamic web apps with HTMX, Python, and Django
item.description: Python is one of the most popular programming language today, in part due to its large ecosystem of tools for data science and AI applications. But Python is also a popular choice for developing web apps, particularly using the Django web framework.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rust memory management explained
item.description: The Rust programming language shares many concepts with other languages intended for systems programming. For instance, Rust distinguishes between memory allocated from the stack and memory allocated from the heap.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco doubles down on AI with new, updated certifications
item.description: Cisco this week furthered its commitment to help customers support and develop artificial intelligence systems by rolling out new certification and training courses aimed at teaching professionals everything from how to incorporate AI into specific roles to advanced networking design.
item.dc:creator: Network World
